title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After receiving an unusual letter from Maupin, Lucy Ashebrook meets Miss Marple in a dark London office. Miss Marple recruits Lucy to conduct investigative work regarding a woman who was strangled on a train. While the police found no evidence of a crime, Miss Marple hypothesizes that the murderer threw the body from the train onto a high embankment near a sharp bend before Blakenham.

Miss Marple identifies the location as Rutherford Manor, a large estate built in 1884 by a manufacturer named Crickenshaw, which is currently inhabited by Crickenshaw's elderly son and daughter. Miss Marple instructs Lucy to secure domestic work at the manor so she can search the embankment for physical evidence, such as damaged shrubs or fabric fragments. Miss Marple warns that the task is dangerous, as the killer managed to commit the crime without leaving a trace or arousing suspicion. To facilitate the plan, Lucy will claim she is working near her elderly aunt, who will stay with Miss Marple's maid, Florence, in Bramhampton.

Lucy agrees to a three-week commitment and successfully secures a position at Rutherford Hall through an employment agency in Bramhampton, which contacts Miss Emma Crickenshaw. Two days later, Lucy drives to the estate. Upon arrival, she observes a grand, castle-like house with neglected stone steps and an abandoned-looking cottage. She is met by a shabby woman in an apron who recognizes her from Miss Marple's recommendation and leads her into a dim hall and a well-furnished sitting room.
